title: Data Acquisition & Environment
text:
Foundational phase combining data acquisition, probe coverage validation, and reproducible environment setup. Confirms the raw datasets contain the variants, samples, and coverage needed — and that the full pipeline is reproducible from a single docker build command.
Timeline: Weeks 1–3

title: Integration & Machine Learning
text:
Unified phase combining unsupervised multi-omics factor analysis (MOFA2, DIABLO) with supervised machine learning (XGBoost + SHAP), a VAE benchmark (expected to underperform classical methods at small N), and an LLM-based PubMed RAG interpretation agent.
Timeline: Weeks 9–15

title: Chromatin Accessibility
text:
Single-nucleus ATAC-seq analysis from postmortem orbitofrontal cortex (GSE256207, n=92) with companion snRNA-seq (GSE254569) for cell-type label transfer. Focused on chromatin accessibility near pharmacogenomically relevant genes and pathway-level concordance with Phase 3 DEGs.
Note: GSE256207 samples are different individuals from the SMRI cohort — only pathway-level concordance is possible, not sample-level integration.
Timeline: Weeks 7–10 (parallelizable with Phase 3)

title: Projects
text:
Pharmacogenomic analysis of psychiatric brain transcriptomics using fully open-access data. This project integrates CYP2C19 activity scores (primary) and CYP2D6 scores (exploratory) with multi-region RNA-seq and postmortem scATAC-seq data.
Strategy: CYP2C19-primary (reliable SNP-based calling) · CYP2D6-secondary (CNV limitations documented)
Demonstrates: End-to-end multi-omics pipeline execution from raw data to interpretable biological insights.
Methods: PharmCAT · DESeq2 · MOFA2 · DIABLO · XGBoost · SHAP · PyTorch · Docker
Full Code: [GitHub]












Downloading and validating SMRI, GEO, and reference datasets alongside a fully reproducible Docker + Snakemake computational environment.














CYP2C19-primary / CYP2D6-exploratory star allele calling from SMRI SNP arrays using PharmCAT, generating continuous activity scores for 105 brains.














Multi-region brain RNA-seq differential expression using DESeq2 with CYP2C19 as the primary continuous covariate across up to 7 brain regions.














Single-nucleus ATAC-seq from postmortem OFC (GSE256207) analyzing pharmacogene-proximal regulatory regions with cell-type resolution.














MOFA2/DIABLO multi-omics integration combined with XGBoost + SHAP predictive modeling, VAE benchmarking, and LLM-based interpretation.














Evidence integration across all phases, publication-quality figures, honest limitations documentation, and portfolio finalization.







No matching items

title: Welcome!
text: This blog documents my learning journey as I build skills in bioinformatics, computational biology, and data science. Here you’ll find problem solutions, analysis walkthroughs, tutorials, and reflections on concepts I’m learning.

title: About Me
text:
Biotechnology, M.S. (incomplete)  University of Central Florida | 2024–2025
Biomedical Sciences, B.S.  Minor in Genomics & Bioinformatics | Minor in Anthropology  University of Central Florida | 2019–2023

title: About Me
text:
Biotechnology, M.S. (incomplete)  University of Central Florida | 2024–2025
Biomedical Sciences, B.S.  Minor in Genomics & Bioinformatics | Minor in Anthropology  University of Central Florida | 2019–2023

title: About Me
text:
My Journey
My interest in bioinformatics emerged from a fascination with how computational approaches can decode biological complexity. With a foundation in biomedical sciences and genomics, I’m now focused on building strong programming and data analysis skills to bridge wet-lab biology and computational research.
I’m working through bioinformatics problem sets, building analysis pipelines, and documenting my learning journey through this portfolio. My background includes teaching experience in gymnastics and education, which has strengthened my ability to break down complex concepts and communicate effectively.

title: About Me
text:
Technical Skills

Programming Languages

Python (primary focus for bioinformatics)
R (statistical analysis, DESeq2, MOFA2, mixOmics)
Bash/Shell scripting
SQL for database querying



Bioinformatics & Multi-Omics

Pharmacogenomic genotyping (PharmCAT, CYP2C19/CYP2D6 activity scoring)
Differential expression analysis (DESeq2 with continuous covariates)
Single-cell ATAC-seq (ArchR/Signac, chromVAR, MACS2)
Multi-omics integration (MOFA2, DIABLO/mixOmics)
Pathway enrichment (clusterProfiler: GO, KEGG, Reactome)



Machine Learning & Data Science

XGBoost with SHAP interpretability
PyTorch (VAE benchmarking)
Optuna hyperparameter optimization
LLM-based interpretation pipelines (RAG with PubMed)



DevOps & Reproducibility

Docker containerization (Bioconductor + Python multi-stage builds)
Snakemake workflow orchestration
Git/GitHub version control + GitHub Actions CI
Linux/WSL development environment
Quarto for reproducible research and portfolio

title: About Me
text:
Interests Beyond Science


Scuba Diving
Open-water and nitrox certified. Diving demands methodical preparation, situational awareness, and staying calm under pressure — skills that translate directly to research and problem solving.


Martial Arts
Black belt in Taekwondo, with training in Muay Thai and Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu. Martial arts taught me that mastery is a process, not a destination — a philosophy I carry into learning bioinformatics.


Yu-Gi-Oh
Competitive player focused on deck building and strategic play. The game demands pattern recognition, probabilistic thinking, and adapting under pressure.


Writing
Currently writing Useless Man: Embracing the Exiled Self — a philosophical exploration of identity, purpose, and the freedom found in not fitting the expected mold.

title: About Me
text:
What’s Next
I’m actively seeking opportunities in bioinformatics, computational biology, or data science where I can apply my growing technical skills and contribute to meaningful research. I’m particularly interested in pharmacogenomics, multi-omics integration, and data-driven biological discovery.
Open to: Full-time positions, internships, collaborations, and freelance projects
Download Resume Download CV

title: Building Computational Tools for Biological Discovery
text:
Multi-omics pipeline developer focused on neuropharmacogenomics, brain transcriptomics, and reproducible bioinformatics — using open-access data and modern ML/integration methods.
Current project: NeuroPGx-MultiOmics — CYP2C19-primary pharmacogenomic stratification of psychiatric brain RNA-seq and scATAC-seq using MOFA2, XGBoost + SHAP, and VAE benchmarking.
View Projects GitHub LinkedIn

NeuroPGx-MultiOmics
Pharmacogenomic stratification of psychiatric brain transcriptomics using SMRI open-access data. Integrates CYP2C19 activity scores (primary) and CYP2D6 scores (exploratory) with multi-region RNA-seq and scATAC-seq through MOFA2, DIABLO, and supervised ML.

Undergraduate research in molecular neuroscience and bioinformatics — including cardiac neuroanatomy, RNA-protein binding kinetics, and microbiome analysis of parasite-infected ants.

title: Research
text: Undergraduate research experience spanning wet-lab neuroscience, molecular biology, and computational genomics. This background informs my current focus on building reproducible multi-omics pipelines for pharmacogenomic analysis.

title: Research
text:
Undergraduate Research

Digitization and Quantification of CGRP-IR Axons in Cardiac Nervous System
Dr. Zixi Cheng | Department of Biomedical Sciences | UCF
June 2022 – May 2023

Performed 3D neuron reconstruction and quantification of calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP) immunoreactive axons in mouse cardiac tissue using confocal microscopy and computational image analysis. Created the first 2D/3D digitized tracings of CGRP-IR axons across whole mouse heart chambers, expanding limited existing datasets on cardiac nociceptive innervation patterns.

Key contributions: - Immunohistochemical staining and tissue preparation for flat-mount imaging - 3D axonal tracing using Neurolucida software across four cardiac chambers - Quantitative analysis pipeline development in ImageJ/Fiji - Data visualization and statistical analysis in GraphPad Prism
View Poster
Methods: Confocal microscopy · Neurolucida · ImageJ · GraphPad Prism · Adobe Illustrator



Mutant U1A-RNA Binding Kinetics with Surface Plasmon Resonance
Dr. Robert Borgon | Department of Biomedical Sciences | UCF
August 2022 – May 2023

Characterized binding kinetics of U1A protein mutants to U1 hairpin II RNA using surface plasmon resonance (SPR). Identified functionally significant amino acid residues in the RNA recognition motif by comparing kinetic constants (ka, kd, KD) across wildtype and point mutant variants.

Key contributions: - Recombinant protein expression and purification with His-tag affinity chromatography - SPR kinetic measurements on NTA sensor chips - Kinetic modeling and curve fitting in TraceDrawer - Comparative proteomic analysis of RRM domains across U1 snRNP subunits
View Poster
Methods: Surface plasmon resonance · PCR · Bacterial culture · R · TraceDrawer · GraphPad Prism



Microbiome Analysis of Ophiocordyceps-Infected Ants
Dr. Anna Forsman | Genomics & Bioinformatics | UCF
January 2022 – May 2022

Analyzed gut microbiome composition differences between carpenter ants infected with behavior-manipulating fungi (Ophiocordyceps), non-manipulating pathogenic fungi (Beauveria), and controls. Performed gDNA extraction, two-step PCR amplification, Illumina sequencing, and taxonomic profiling via QIIME 2 to assess microbial community shifts associated with fungal infection.

Methods: Illumina sequencing · PCR · QIIME 2 · Gel electrophoresis

title: Research
text:
Research Direction
Current work focuses on neuropharmacogenomics — specifically, how genetic variation in drug metabolism enzymes (CYP2D6, CYP2C19) correlates with brain transcriptomic and epigenomic profiles in psychiatric disorders. This builds on wet-lab molecular biology foundations while developing the computational skills (multi-omics integration, reproducible pipelines, machine learning) needed for independent research in this area.
The NeuroPGx-MultiOmics project represents the transition from supporting wet-lab research to designing and executing full computational biology analyses end-to-end.
View Current Project →

title: Brain Transcriptomics
text:
Differential gene expression analysis on SMRI Stanley RNA-seq data (up to 7 brain regions) using CYP2C19 activity score as the primary continuous covariate in DESeq2. CYP2D6 run as a separate exploratory model.
Timeline: Weeks 5–8

title: Synthesis & Reporting
text:
Ties all previous phases into a coherent narrative. Core question: does CYP2C19 metabolizer status correlate with detectable transcriptomic signatures in postmortem psychiatric brain, and is there convergent evidence from chromatin accessibility?
Timeline: Weeks 14–17

title: PGx Genotyping
text:
CYP2C19 and CYP2D6 star allele calling from SMRI Affymetrix SNP 5.0 data using PharmCAT. Implements a CYP2C19-primary / CYP2D6-secondary strategy — CYP2C19 is reliably called from SNP arrays, while CYP2D6 is exploratory due to structural variation (deletions, duplications, CYP2D7 hybrids) invisible to arrays.
Timeline: Weeks 3–5
